sourceShortSummaries: The session opens with the party camped west of Tollen as the players reintroduce the five adventurers and their relationships.
sourceLongSummaries: The group is traveling with Alden toward an old house after being hired in Tollen to make sure a minor lord's new property is safe. Around the campfire, Txarro, Cato Min, Tarek, Edric, and Ayveen are described in turn, with the conversation establishing Txarro's rescue from tree blights, Cato and Tarek's carny and pickpocket routines, Edric's Night Queen faith, Ayveen's bardic optimism, and the party's shared identity as an affectionate band of misfits.

sourceEntries.title: Campfire Introductions
sourceEntries.boundaryReason: Opening setup and the full campfire round of PC descriptions and party relationship backstory; the next beat begins when Alden shifts the conversation to their previous job.
sourceEntries.shortSummary: The session opens with the party camped west of Tollen as the players reintroduce the five adventurers and their relationships.
sourceEntries.longSummary: The group is traveling with Alden toward an old house after being hired in Tollen to make sure a minor lord's new property is safe. Around the campfire, Txarro, Cato Min, Tarek, Edric, and Ayveen are described in turn, with the conversation establishing Txarro's rescue from tree blights, Cato and Tarek's carny and pickpocket routines, Edric's Night Queen faith, Ayveen's bardic optimism, and the party's shared identity as an affectionate band of misfits.

locationHints.context: campfire on the last night before reaching Dandelion House
locationHints.notes: Alden expects the party to reach Dandelion House by sunset the next day.

npcDetails.name: Alden
npcDetails.context: guide and agent for the minor lord
npcDetails.notes: He is traveling with the party and has recruited them for the job.

sourceShortSummaries: Alden asks the party about their previous job, reveals his love of Dandelion House, and is quietly investigated before the night watch.
sourceLongSummaries: Alden prompts each adventurer to describe a favorite part of the prior job, when an elf in Tollen hired them to recover old books from an ettin and its allies. Their answers highlight Cato's appetite for easy profit, Edric's relief when enemies flee, Tarek's fixation on salvageable loot, Txarro's enjoyment of fighting as a tiger, and Ayveen's faith in teamwork. Alden then talks rapturously about Dandelion House and dandelions, Cato uses Detect Thoughts and senses genuine joy, delight, longing, and nostalgia, and the party checks Alden's gear, sets watches, and turns in after Edric prays to the Night Queen.

sourceEntries.title: Alden Asks About Last Job
sourceEntries.boundaryReason: Alden prompts the group to recount their last job, reveals his devotion to Dandelion House, and the party probes him before setting watches; the next beat starts with the morning departure.
sourceEntries.shortSummary: Alden asks the party about their previous job, reveals his love of Dandelion House, and is quietly investigated before the night watch.
sourceEntries.longSummary: Alden prompts each adventurer to describe a favorite part of the prior job, when an elf in Tollen hired them to recover old books from an ettin and its allies. Their answers highlight Cato's appetite for easy profit, Edric's relief when enemies flee, Tarek's fixation on salvageable loot, Txarro's enjoyment of fighting as a tiger, and Ayveen's faith in teamwork. Alden then talks rapturously about Dandelion House and dandelions, Cato uses Detect Thoughts and senses genuine joy, delight, longing, and nostalgia, and the party checks Alden's gear, sets watches, and turns in after Edric prays to the Night Queen.

locationHints.context: same campfire during the night before arrival
locationHints.notes: The beat remains at the campsite until the watches pass uneventfully.

npcDetails.name: Alden
npcDetails.notes: He asks about the previous job and shows intense affection for Dandelion House and dandelions.

npcDetails.name: unnamed elf patron
npcDetails.notes: The elf in Tollen hired the party to recover old books from the ettin's hoard.

npcDetails.name: ettin
npcDetails.notes: The two-headed giant had the books and turned the tables when the party tried to sneak up on it.

itemDetails.name: old books
itemDetails.notes: Recovered for the elf patron during the previous job.

sourceShortSummaries: The party travels through a trackless old forest and reaches the overgrown grounds of Dandelion House at dusk.
sourceLongSummaries: After an uneventful watch, the party breaks camp and follows Alden through increasingly dense old woodland where the path vanishes, wildlife dwindles, and even Moss Foot becomes more alert. They press through lunch to arrive before dark, pass a ruined wall, gate, moat, roots, and dandelion imagery, and see a majestic but decrepit manor still standing in the forest. Alden warns them that Lord Holda is sensitive, disinherited, and requires patience and flattery, Colden takes Moss Foot to the stables, and the party uses magical checks, history, and cautious observation before finally agreeing to enter.

sourceEntries.title: Road To Dandelion House
sourceEntries.boundaryReason: The party breaks camp, travels through the increasingly strange forest, reaches Dandelion House, and hesitates at the threshold; the next beat begins as they enter the house.
sourceEntries.shortSummary: The party travels through a trackless old forest and reaches the overgrown grounds of Dandelion House at dusk.
sourceEntries.longSummary: After an uneventful watch, the party breaks camp and follows Alden through increasingly dense old woodland where the path vanishes, wildlife dwindles, and even Moss Foot becomes more alert. They press through lunch to arrive before dark, pass a ruined wall, gate, moat, roots, and dandelion imagery, and see a majestic but decrepit manor still standing in the forest. Alden warns them that Lord Holda is sensitive, disinherited, and requires patience and flattery, Colden takes Moss Foot to the stables, and the party uses magical checks, history, and cautious observation before finally agreeing to enter.

locationHints.context: long day's hike through increasingly trackless forest to the manor threshold
locationHints.notes: The beat begins the morning after camp and ends just before the party enters the house.

npcDetails.name: Alden
npcDetails.notes: He keeps a steady pace, appears to know the route, and briefs the party on Lord Holda.

npcDetails.name: Lord Holda
npcDetails.notes: Alden says Holda is difficult, proud, and desperate for the house to fulfill his lifelong dream.

npcDetails.name: Colden
npcDetails.notes: He appears when Alden calls and takes Moss Foot toward the stables.

npcDetails.name: Moss Foot
npcDetails.notes: The pony becomes more alert during the strange approach and is taken to the stables.

itemDetails.name: dandelion crest
itemDetails.notes: A dandelion puffball motif is visible above the manor door.

organizationDetails.name: Drankorian Empire
organizationDetails.notes: The manor is identified as a very late Drankorian architectural work.

sourceShortSummaries: Inside Dandelion House, the party examines the preserved but unsettling manor and meets Lord Holda before dinner.
sourceLongSummaries: Alden leads the group through the dusty entry hall, where paths through the dust, magical preservation, old rooms, a grandfather clock, and garden-themed tapestries make the house feel both magnificent and ominous. Edric's detection spell finds no fey or undead nearby, Tarek checks tracks in the dust, and Cato grows increasingly uneasy about the house's empty, party-haunted grandeur. On the third floor, the party finds Lord Holda in the last golden light, hears Alden formally present them, and then follows Holda down to the kitchen, where preserved magical conveniences and a cleaning bar are used before dinner.

sourceEntries.title: Inside The Manor
sourceEntries.boundaryReason: The group enters Dandelion House, explores the dusty preserved interior, ascends to meet Lord Holda, and gets seated and cleaned before dinner; the next beat starts with Holda's substantive explanation at the table.
sourceEntries.shortSummary: Inside Dandelion House, the party examines the preserved but unsettling manor and meets Lord Holda before dinner.
sourceEntries.longSummary: Alden leads the group through the dusty entry hall, where paths through the dust, magical preservation, old rooms, a grandfather clock, and garden-themed tapestries make the house feel both magnificent and ominous. Edric's detection spell finds no fey or undead nearby, Tarek checks tracks in the dust, and Cato grows increasingly uneasy about the house's empty, party-haunted grandeur. On the third floor, the party finds Lord Holda in the last golden light, hears Alden formally present them, and then follows Holda down to the kitchen, where preserved magical conveniences and a cleaning bar are used before dinner.

locationHints.context: entry hall, upper floors, observatory, and kitchen
locationHints.notes: The house is intact but overgrown, magically preserved in places, and increasingly dark as evening falls.

npcDetails.name: Alden
npcDetails.notes: He leads the party through the house and introduces them to Lord Holda.

npcDetails.name: Lord Holda
npcDetails.notes: He is a scarred, dangerous-looking former adventurer who appraises the party before inviting them to eat.

itemDetails.name: cleaning bar
itemDetails.notes: A magical iron bar in the kitchen cleans anyone who taps it; Tarek assesses whether it could be removed.

itemDetails.name: magical ice chest
itemDetails.notes: The kitchen contains a cold magical chest used to store food.

itemDetails.name: magical cupboard
itemDetails.notes: A cupboard produces or preserves a fresh loaf of bread.

itemDetails.name: garden tapestries
itemDetails.notes: Faded tapestries depict gardeners working in greenhouses.

sourceShortSummaries: Lord Holda explains the ancient deed and hires the party to end an unknown treaty beyond a basement door.
After Holda leaves, the party debates the suspicious bargain, prepares for rest, and decides to see what lies below.
sourceLongSummaries: Over dinner, Holda recounts decades as an adventurer, a costly victory over a young dragon, and the strange elf who delivered a centuries-old deed naming him heir to Dandelion House, its lands, and its treasure. The deed is also a contract: the house and hoard become his only if he steps through a door in the floor and convinces the man on the other side to end a long-held treaty. Holda admits he failed badly, offers the party half the treasure to succeed for him, and explains that the door opens onto another place with a road and another manor; Tarek reads the Sembaran contract and senses that something about its pieces does not fit, but the party agrees to rest and attempt the job in the morning.

With Holda gone, Cato is wildly excited by the possible infinite treasure while Txarro, Edric, Tarek, and Ayveen question the contract, the unknown treaty, and the possibility that ending it could be dangerous or unrewarding. Alden shows them how the magical sconces work, Edric checks that Moss Foot is safe, Txarro casts Goodberry and hands out supplies, and Tarek pockets a spoon before the group agrees that they have come too far not to look through the trapdoor. The beat ends with the party choosing rest rather than further pilfering or exploration before the morning descent.

sourceEntries.title: Holda Explains The Contract
sourceEntries.boundaryReason: Dinner with Lord Holda covers his adventuring past, the ancient deed, the treaty condition, the other side of the door, and the party's acceptance of the job; the next beat begins with their private reaction before bed.
sourceEntries.shortSummary: Lord Holda explains the ancient deed and hires the party to end an unknown treaty beyond a basement door.
sourceEntries.longSummary: Over dinner, Holda recounts decades as an adventurer, a costly victory over a young dragon, and the strange elf who delivered a centuries-old deed naming him heir to Dandelion House, its lands, and its treasure. The deed is also a contract: the house and hoard become his only if he steps through a door in the floor and convinces the man on the other side to end a long-held treaty. Holda admits he failed badly, offers the party half the treasure to succeed for him, and explains that the door opens onto another place with a road and another manor; Tarek reads the Sembaran contract and senses that something about its pieces does not fit, but the party agrees to rest and attempt the job in the morning.

locationHints.notes: The party remains at the kitchen table while Holda explains the contract and proposed job.

npcDetails.name: Lord Holda
npcDetails.notes: He wants the party to complete the treaty condition that he failed to fulfill.

npcDetails.name: Alden
npcDetails.notes: He brings food and later is assigned to show the party to quarters.

npcDetails.name: unnamed elf archivist
npcDetails.notes: Holda says this elf's organization held the deed in trust until the right time.

npcDetails.name: unnamed lord beyond the door
npcDetails.notes: Holda met him through the basement door but failed to learn much or persuade him.

itemDetails.name: ancient deed and contract
itemDetails.notes: The brittle Sembaran document names Holda and lays out the condition for inheriting Dandelion House.

itemDetails.name: treaty
itemDetails.notes: The party must persuade the unknown man beyond the door to bring a long-held treaty to an end.

itemDetails.name: treasure hoard
itemDetails.notes: Holda promises the party half the value of the gold, coin, jewels, and other treasure if they complete the job.

itemDetails.name: door in the floor
itemDetails.notes: The contract says the doom of the house lies locked beyond this basement door.

organizationDetails.name: unnamed elf organization
organizationDetails.notes: The organization allegedly held the deed in trust for centuries.

sourceEntries.title: Deal And Nighttime Doubts
sourceEntries.boundaryReason: After Holda leaves, the party debates the suspicious contract and possible treasure, handles last errands and supplies, and takes the long rest; the next beat begins when the magical clock wakes them before dawn.
sourceEntries.shortSummary: After Holda leaves, the party debates the suspicious bargain, prepares for rest, and decides to see what lies below.
sourceEntries.longSummary: With Holda gone, Cato is wildly excited by the possible infinite treasure while Txarro, Edric, Tarek, and Ayveen question the contract, the unknown treaty, and the possibility that ending it could be dangerous or unrewarding. Alden shows them how the magical sconces work, Edric checks that Moss Foot is safe, Txarro casts Goodberry and hands out supplies, and Tarek pockets a spoon before the group agrees that they have come too far not to look through the trapdoor. The beat ends with the party choosing rest rather than further pilfering or exploration before the morning descent.

locationHints.context: kitchen and guest quarters after dinner
locationHints.notes: The party remains in the manor for the evening and long rest.

npcDetails.name: Lord Holda
npcDetails.notes: The party debates whether he is trustworthy after he leaves the room.

npcDetails.name: Alden
npcDetails.notes: He points out how the sconces work and is available to show the party to rooms.

npcDetails.name: Moss Foot
npcDetails.notes: Edric wants to check on Moss Foot before bed; the pony is doing fine.

itemDetails.name: ancient deed and contract
itemDetails.notes: The party debates whether the contract protects or endangers them.

itemDetails.name: treaty
itemDetails.notes: Ayveen and others worry that ending it could release something dangerous.

itemDetails.name: treasure hoard
itemDetails.notes: Cato fixates on the possibility of an enormous payout.

itemDetails.name: Goodberries
itemDetails.notes: Txarro creates and distributes two Goodberries per party member for the next day.

itemDetails.name: silver spoon
itemDetails.notes: Tarek takes a spoon during a quick bit of pilfering.

sourceShortSummaries: Before dawn, the party descends below Dandelion House, enters a root tunnel, and is sealed in as Tarek realizes the contract's language is wrong.
sourceLongSummaries: A magical clock wakes the party before dawn, and Alden leads them from their rooms down through the ground floor, basement, and a rougher lower stair opened with a glowing gold key. In a root-covered sub-cellar, Lord Holda waits by a hidden door and tells them to crawl through the dark passage, promising to hold this end open. The party enters in order through what proves to be the hollow center of an enormous dry root, but the door closes behind them; in the same moment, Tarek realizes that the unquestionably 400-year-old contract was written in perfectly contemporary Sembaran, and the session ends with the way back shut.

sourceEntries.title: Into The Root Tunnel
sourceEntries.boundaryReason: The party wakes before dawn, descends through the basement and root cellar, crawls into the enormous root passage, and ends on the door closing behind them.
sourceEntries.shortSummary: Before dawn, the party descends below Dandelion House, enters a root tunnel, and is sealed in as Tarek realizes the contract's language is wrong.
sourceEntries.longSummary: A magical clock wakes the party before dawn, and Alden leads them from their rooms down through the ground floor, basement, and a rougher lower stair opened with a glowing gold key. In a root-covered sub-cellar, Lord Holda waits by a hidden door and tells them to crawl through the dark passage, promising to hold this end open. The party enters in order through what proves to be the hollow center of an enormous dry root, but the door closes behind them; in the same moment, Tarek realizes that the unquestionably 400-year-old contract was written in perfectly contemporary Sembaran, and the session ends with the way back shut.

locationHints.context: pre-dawn descent through the basement and root cellar into the hidden passage
locationHints.notes: The beat ends with the party crawling through the root tunnel after the door behind them closes.

npcDetails.name: Alden
npcDetails.notes: He wakes and collects the party before leading them down.

npcDetails.name: Lord Holda
npcDetails.notes: He stands by the hidden door and says he will keep this end open.

itemDetails.name: gold key
itemDetails.notes: Alden uses a smaller glowing gold key to unlock the lower door.

itemDetails.name: hidden door
itemDetails.notes: The door opens into a narrow root passage and closes behind the party.

itemDetails.name: ancient deed and contract
itemDetails.notes: Tarek realizes the old contract is written in contemporary Sembaran, creating a new contradiction.

organizationDetails.name: Philosopher's Guild
organizationDetails.notes: Txarro wonders why Holda did not take the suspicious contract to the Philosopher's Guild.

sourceEntries.title: Deal And Nighttime Doubts
sourceEntries.boundaryReason: After Holda leaves, the party debates the suspicious contract and possible treasure, handles last errands and supplies, and takes the long rest; the next beat begins when the magical clock wakes them before dawn.
sourceEntries.shortSummary: After Holda leaves, the party debates the suspicious bargain, prepares for rest, and decides to see what lies below.
sourceEntries.longSummary: With Holda gone, Cato is wildly excited by the possible infinite treasure while Txarro, Edric, Tarek, and Ayveen question the contract, the unknown treaty, and the possibility that ending it could be dangerous or unrewarding. Alden shows them how the magical sconces work, Edric checks that Moss Foot is safe, Txarro casts Goodberry and hands out supplies, and Tarek pockets a spoon before the group agrees that they have come too far not to look through the trapdoor. The beat ends with the party choosing rest rather than further pilfering or exploration before the morning descent.

locationHints.context: kitchen and guest quarters after dinner
locationHints.notes: The party remains in the manor for the evening and long rest.

npcDetails.name: Lord Holda
npcDetails.notes: The party debates whether he is trustworthy after he leaves the room.

npcDetails.name: Alden
npcDetails.notes: He points out how the sconces work and is available to show the party to rooms.

npcDetails.name: Moss Foot
npcDetails.notes: Edric wants to check on Moss Foot before bed; the pony is doing fine.

itemDetails.name: ancient deed and contract
itemDetails.notes: The party debates whether the contract protects or endangers them.

itemDetails.name: treaty
itemDetails.notes: Ayveen and others worry that ending it could release something dangerous.

itemDetails.name: treasure hoard
itemDetails.notes: Cato fixates on the possibility of an enormous payout.

itemDetails.name: Goodberries
itemDetails.notes: Txarro creates and distributes two Goodberries per party member for the next day.

itemDetails.name: silver spoon
itemDetails.notes: Tarek takes a spoon during a quick bit of pilfering.

recapBlocks.title: Into The Root Tunnel
sourceShortSummaries: Before dawn, the party descends below Dandelion House, enters a root tunnel, and is sealed in as Tarek realizes the contract's language is wrong.
sourceLongSummaries: A magical clock wakes the party before dawn, and Alden leads them from their rooms down through the ground floor, basement, and a rougher lower stair opened with a glowing gold key. In a root-covered sub-cellar, Lord Holda waits by a hidden door and tells them to crawl through the dark passage, promising to hold this end open. The party enters in order through what proves to be the hollow center of an enormous dry root, but the door closes behind them; in the same moment, Tarek realizes that the unquestionably 400-year-old contract was written in perfectly contemporary Sembaran, and the session ends with the way back shut.

sourceEntries.title: Into The Root Tunnel
sourceEntries.boundaryReason: The party wakes before dawn, descends through the basement and root cellar, crawls into the enormous root passage, and ends on the door closing behind them.
sourceEntries.shortSummary: Before dawn, the party descends below Dandelion House, enters a root tunnel, and is sealed in as Tarek realizes the contract's language is wrong.
sourceEntries.longSummary: A magical clock wakes the party before dawn, and Alden leads them from their rooms down through the ground floor, basement, and a rougher lower stair opened with a glowing gold key. In a root-covered sub-cellar, Lord Holda waits by a hidden door and tells them to crawl through the dark passage, promising to hold this end open. The party enters in order through what proves to be the hollow center of an enormous dry root, but the door closes behind them; in the same moment, Tarek realizes that the unquestionably 400-year-old contract was written in perfectly contemporary Sembaran, and the session ends with the way back shut.

locationHints.context: pre-dawn descent through the basement and root cellar into the hidden passage
locationHints.notes: The beat ends with the party crawling through the root tunnel after the door behind them closes.

npcDetails.name: Alden
npcDetails.notes: He wakes and collects the party before leading them down.

npcDetails.name: Lord Holda
npcDetails.notes: He stands by the hidden door and says he will keep this end open.

itemDetails.name: gold key
itemDetails.notes: Alden uses a smaller glowing gold key to unlock the lower door.

itemDetails.name: hidden door
itemDetails.notes: The door opens into a narrow root passage and closes behind the party.

itemDetails.name: ancient deed and contract
itemDetails.notes: Tarek realizes the old contract is written in contemporary Sembaran, creating a new contradiction.

organizationDetails.name: Philosopher's Guild
organizationDetails.notes: Txarro wonders why Holda did not take the suspicious contract to the Philosopher's Guild.

npcs.name: Alden
npcs.contextHint: guide and agent for the minor lord
